d5e1_health_inspection.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_health_inspection.text: Day 5! Your final day. Everything you've built comes down to this. A health inspector arrives unannounced this morning. How prepared are you?

d5e1_inspector_b.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_inspector_b.text: Good morning. We received a complaint about your laundromat's cleanliness. I'll need to do a full inspection right now. Are you ready?

choices.text: Welcome them confidently — your laundromat is always inspection ready
Ask for a few minutes to tidy up quickly
Cooperate fully and answer all questions honestly
Ask to see their credentials first

d5e1_ready.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_ready.text: Impressive. Everything is spotless. Your standards are well above average. Consider this a passed inspection.

d5e1_stall.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_stall.text: A few minutes? That's irregular. I'm noting this hesitation in my report.

d5e1_cooperate.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_cooperate.text: Thank you for your transparency. Everything checks out. You run a clean and honest operation here.

d5e1_verify.speaker: Health Inspector
d5e1_verify.text: Of course. Here are my credentials. The complaint was about machine cleanliness. Shall we proceed?

d5e1_result_ready.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_ready.text: Maintaining standards every day — not just for inspections — is what separates great laundromats from average ones. Well done.

d5e1_result_stall.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_stall.text: If you need time to tidy up for an inspection, your daily standards need work. Cleanliness should be a habit, not a reaction.

d5e1_result_cooperate.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_cooperate.text: Transparency with regulators builds institutional trust. Honesty during inspections protects you more than hiding problems ever could.

d5e1_result_verify.speaker: Mentor
d5e1_result_verify.text: Knowing your rights and asking for credentials is professional and smart. Always verify authority before granting access.

d5e2_pr_crisis.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_pr_crisis.text: Midday crisis! A social media post is going viral claiming your laundromat ruined a customer's expensive clothes. The post is spreading fast.

d5e2_crisis_b.speaker: You
d5e2_crisis_b.text: This is a full-blown PR crisis. Every second I wait makes it worse. I need to act now...

choices.text: Release a public statement immediately
Investigate internally before saying anything
Reach out directly to the customer who posted
Offer to replace the damaged clothes immediately

d5e2_statement.speaker: Online User
d5e2_statement.text: They addressed it immediately and took responsibility. That's actually really mature. Respect.

d5e2_investigate.speaker: Online User
d5e2_investigate.text: It's been hours and they haven't said anything. The silence is making them look guilty.

d5e2_direct.speaker: Customer
d5e2_direct.text: They actually messaged me personally. That took guts. I appreciate that more than a generic post.

d5e2_replace.speaker: Customer
d5e2_replace.text: They're replacing my clothes? That's above and beyond. I'm updating my post to say they made it right.

d5e2_result_statement.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_statement.text: Swift, transparent communication during a crisis preserves trust. Silence lets others control the narrative about your business.

d5e2_result_investigate.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_investigate.text: Internal investigation is important, but so is acknowledging the issue publicly while you look into it. Both can happen simultaneously.

d5e2_result_direct.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_direct.text: Direct personal outreach during a crisis shows humanity. People remember how you made them feel far longer than what you said.

d5e2_result_replace.speaker: Mentor
d5e2_result_replace.text: Generous service recovery turns a critic into an advocate. The cost hurts now but buys reputation that no marketing can replace.

d5e3_final_partnership.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_final_partnership.text: Final major event! A long-time customer approaches you with a proposal to become a business partner and co-invest in expanding your laundromat.

d5e3_partner_b.speaker: Long-Time Customer
d5e3_partner_b.text: I've watched you grow this laundromat with my own eyes. I want to invest and help you open a second location. What do you say?

choices.text: Accept enthusiastically — this is the opportunity you've been waiting for
Ask for a formal proposal first
Propose a smaller pilot expansion first
Decline — focus on strengthening what you already have

d5e3_accept.speaker: Long-Time Customer
d5e3_accept.text: Fantastic! This is going to be incredible. Two laundromats — let's change the neighborhood together!

d5e3_review.speaker: Long-Time Customer
d5e3_review.text: Smart thinking. I'll have my team prepare a full proposal by end of week.

d5e3_pilot.speaker: Long-Time Customer
d5e3_pilot.text: A pilot? Test before you scale. You've learned a lot this week, haven't you?

d5e3_decline.speaker: Long-Time Customer
d5e3_decline.text: I respect that. Knowing when not to expand is just as important as knowing when to grow.

d5e3_result_accept.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_accept.text: Exciting times ahead! But always get everything in writing before celebrating. A handshake deal is not a business deal.

d5e3_result_review.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_review.text: Never let excitement override due diligence. Reviewing proposals protects you from partnerships that look good but lack substance.

d5e3_result_pilot.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_pilot.text: Testing before scaling is one of the most valuable lessons in business. You've truly grown this week.

d5e3_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d5e3_result_decline.text: Sustainable growth beats reckless expansion every time. Consolidating your foundation before scaling is wisdom, not fear.

d5r_last_minute_customer.speaker: Customer
d5r_last_minute_customer.text: I know you're closing soon, but I have an emergency. My washer broke at home and I have work clothes for tomorrow. Can you help?

d5r_customer_b.speaker: You
d5r_customer_b.text: A last-minute customer on your final day. How do you respond?

choices.text: Stay late to help them — kindness matters
Refer them to another laundromat
Charge extra for after-hours service

d5r_customer_help.speaker: Customer
d5r_customer_help.text: You stayed late for me? You've earned a customer for life. Thank you so much.

d5r_customer_refer.speaker: Customer
d5r_customer_refer.text: I drove all the way here. This is really disappointing.

d5r_customer_extra.speaker: Customer
d5r_customer_extra.text: Extra charge for staying late? I understand, but it doesn't feel great.

d5r_customer_result_help.speaker: Mentor
d5r_customer_result_help.text: Going above and beyond on your final day leaves a lasting impression. That customer will tell everyone about your kindness.

d5r_customer_result_refer.speaker: Mentor
d5r_customer_result_refer.text: Sending customers away on your last day misses a final chance to build loyalty. Sometimes the last interaction matters most.

d5r_customer_result_extra.speaker: Mentor
d5r_customer_result_extra.text: Capitalizing on emergencies may profit today but cost you tomorrow. People remember when you helped them versus when you charged them.

d5r_employee_bonus_request.speaker: Staff
d5r_employee_bonus_request.text: Boss, the team worked really hard this week. We were wondering if there's any chance of a bonus for the final day?

d5r_bonus_b.speaker: You
d5r_bonus_b.text: Your team has worked hard all week. How do you reward them?

choices.text: Give everyone a generous bonus
Give a modest bonus with thank-you notes
Promise a bonus after results are calculated
Thank them but no bonus this week

d5r_bonus_generous.speaker: Staff
d5r_bonus_generous.text: A generous bonus? You're the best boss. We'll never forget this.

d5r_bonus_modest.speaker: Staff
d5r_bonus_modest.text: A bonus AND handwritten notes? That's really thoughtful. Thank you.

d5r_bonus_promise.speaker: Staff
d5r_bonus_promise.text: We'll hold you to that promise. Looking forward to hearing the results.

d5r_bonus_none.speaker: Staff
d5r_bonus_none.text: A simple thank you... we worked really hard this week. A little disappointing.

d5r_bonus_result_generous.speaker: Mentor
d5r_bonus_result_generous.text: Generous rewards create loyal teams who will go above and beyond for you. The cost today buys commitment for the future.

d5r_bonus_result_modest.speaker: Mentor
d5r_bonus_result_modest.text: Modest rewards paired with genuine appreciation can be as meaningful as larger ones. The personal touch matters.

d5r_bonus_result_promise.speaker: Mentor
d5r_bonus_result_promise.text: Promises work only if you keep them. Make sure you follow through or you'll lose the trust you've built.

d5r_bonus_result_none.speaker: Mentor
d5r_bonus_result_none.text: Failing to reward hard work, especially when you can afford it, demotivates your team. Recognition matters, even if small.

d5r_supplier_final_deal.speaker: Supplier
d5r_supplier_final_deal.text: Congrats on making it through the week! As a thank-you, we want to offer you a special bulk deal on premium detergent.

d5r_supplier_b.speaker: You
d5r_supplier_b.text: A final supplier offer. How do you handle this opportunity?

choices.text: Accept immediately — stock up for success
Negotiate an even better deal
Decline — you have enough supplies

d5r_supplier_accept.speaker: Supplier
d5r_supplier_accept.text: Excellent! Your supply closet will be stocked for months. Pleasure doing business with you.

d5r_supplier_negotiate.speaker: Supplier
d5r_supplier_negotiate.text: You drive a hard bargain. Deal! It's been great working with a smart business owner.

d5r_supplier_decline.speaker: Supplier
d5r_supplier_decline.text: Fair enough. The offer stands if you change your mind. Good luck with everything!

d5r_supplier_result_accept.speaker: Mentor
d5r_supplier_result_accept.text: Seizing supplier opportunities when they come shows confidence in your future. Stocking up when you can is smart business.

d5r_supplier_result_negotiate.speaker: Mentor
d5r_supplier_result_negotiate.text: Negotiating even at the last minute shows you never stop looking for value. That mindset will serve you well.

d5r_supplier_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d5r_supplier_result_decline.text: Knowing when you have enough is wisdom. Not every opportunity is right for every moment.

d5e5_reflection.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_reflection.text: Final event. The week is over. You made it through all five days. Looking back, what's the most important lesson you'll take forward?

d5e5_reflection_b.speaker: You
d5e5_reflection_b.text: This week taught me so much about running a business. What should I focus on going forward?

choices.text: Building strong customer relationships
Creating better operational systems
Investing in my team's growth
Balancing work and personal wellbeing

d5e5_customers.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_customers.text: Customer relationships are the foundation of any business. You've learned that people choose places where they feel valued. Keep that at the center.

d5e5_systems.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_systems.text: Good systems let you scale without burning out. You've seen how processes can save you during crisis. Build more of them.

d5e5_team.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_team.text: Your team is your greatest asset. You've learned that investing in people pays returns you can't measure on a spreadsheet. Never stop developing them.

d5e5_wellbeing.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_wellbeing.text: You can't pour from an empty cup. The entrepreneurs who last are the ones who learn to balance ambition with rest. Protect that insight.

d5e5_result_customers.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_result_customers.text: You've completed the week with wisdom that will serve you for years. Your business journey is just beginning. Well done.

d5e5_result_systems.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_result_systems.text: You've learned one of the most scalable lessons in business. Systems turn chaos into consistency. Your future self will thank you.

d5e5_result_team.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_result_team.text: Leaders who prioritize their people build businesses that last. You've learned the secret that many never figure out.

d5e5_result_wellbeing.speaker: Mentor
d5e5_result_wellbeing.text: The most valuable lesson is learning to care for yourself. A burned-out owner cannot lead anyone. You've learned what matters most.
